title: uragoga regnellii
intro: Uragoga regnellii, commonly known as the 'Pau-Brasil' or 'Brazilian Rosewood', is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Uragoga regnellii are attributed to its therapeutic actions, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which are derived from its bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. These bioactive compounds contribute to the plant's potential in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.
side_effects: Uragoga regnellii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged consumption can also lead to liver and kidney damage. In some cases, excessive use of the plant has been linked to cardiovascular problems, including high blood pressure and irregular heartbeat. Furthermore, improper usage may cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and respiratory issues.
